posts.title: U.S. Forest Service ignites new controversy with record glyphosate spraying on burned public lands - Willow Tohi

link_metadata.description: The U.S. Forest Service is spraying record amounts of glyphosate on fire-damaged public lands across California, with 266,000 pounds applied in 2023 alone — five times the amount sprayed 20 years ago. The agency uses the herbicide to kill native vegetation that competes with commercially valuable conifer species replanted after wildfires. A key study used […]
